title: geophila gracilis
intro: Geophila gracilis, also known as creeping Jenny, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the primrose family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Geophila gracilis include its ability to treat eczema, acne, and other skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Geophila gracilis can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
